customManagers.description: Version pins annotated with a `# renovate:` comment in the Python constants, the legacy script, and workflow shell blocks (pypi / github-releases / github-tags).
sunxi-tools commit pins (Python constants, legacy script, both build workflows), tracked on the master branch.
Docker images pinned tag@digest inside a workflow `docker run` shell block.

packageRules.description: Automerge patch, minor, and digest updates. Every dep that ships or builds the tool is exercised by a CI job (shellcheck / python / build / integration) and Renovate only automerges on green, so a breaking bump fails the PR before it can merge. Major updates, and the exceptions below, are reviewed by hand.
These actions run ONLY inside the release/publish workflows, which ci.yml's push checks don't exercise, so their minor + major bumps get a human review (patch + digest still automerge).

Valetudo is the firmware flashed to the robot. Renovate surfaces version bumps (the download self-verifies against GitHub's published digest, so there's no checksum to hand-edit), but never automerge them: a human should eye each firmware change.
CPython (the interpreter frozen into the bundle): patch bumps automerge on green; a NEW minor (e.g. 3.14 -> 3.15) is a bigger move that gets a human review.
prBodyNotes: A CPython minor bump must also update `depends_on "python@3.x"` in packaging/homebrew/dreame-valetudo.rb by hand — no Renovate manager covers the formula.

packageRules.description: The .deb builder image (docker `python`) freezes the same interpreter as the bundle; a MINOR bump (3.14 -> 3.15) must not slip in via automerge — it gets the same human review as the setup-python CPython pin.
